name: Model Graphviz
description: Reusable SVG visual contract for TorchVista/Graphviz model architecture maps, report overlays, and evidence/focus overlays. Captures the approved DeepSeek V3.2 graph style and bundles clean architecture schema assets for DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen7B, openPangu-2.0-Flash, and local Pangu MoE variants; the legacy Gemma schema and openPangu Ultra-MoE 718B schema remain available as assets but are excluded from the preview gallery. The contract keeps default depth-2 folding, no default selected operator, pill module/op nodes, rounded-rect tensor/state nodes, transparent parent cluster shells with a fixed --radius-xl (16px) corner for every container including repeated layer templates, white edges, vertically centered node label+type stacks, the shared model-architecture op-category color profile for gallery model previews with communication operators in a blue-green lane, left-side Parameter/Weight input nodes, right-side State/Cache auxiliary nodes, semantic-first colormap defaults for common op roles with unknown keys expanded through the shared palette, red/green forbidden hue ranges in generated palettes, stronger light-mode node fills with no default op/module node border, strict semantic module hierarchy, explicit op semantic colors before parent fallback, folded representative edge dedupe, edge tag graph-node collision avoidance with perpendicular fallback offsets, stable expand/collapse viewport anchoring, centered report-priority container title pills, no-extra-border priority tags, top-right corner fold controls, right-inset expand controls, no graph shadows, and no-border clicked-operator preview cards with dark code surfaces. The base renderer also owns optional controller behavior formerly duplicated by model-training-graphviz: evidence hover panels, edge-object hover panels for tagged tensor/parameter/activation flows, edge type tags, selected/related emphasis, hierarchy-aware focus, drag pan, Command-wheel zoom, and stable non-refitting phase/focus changes. New model graph pages should derive cluster boxes from the bounding box of their visible children while reserving cluster-title and external parameter-edge clearance, lay the decoder chain out as a single top-to-bottom column (Gate/Up the only parallel pair), use transform-based cursor-anchored pan/zoom through renderController when interaction is needed, show a single neutral selection stroke with no browser focus ring only after user selection, and place a repeat-count badge at the cluster top-left under the title.

source.parityNote: The pattern preview embeds the bundled final DeepSeek V3.2 Graphviz page with an iframe for parity. The Graphviz page still owns DOT generation, Graphviz layout, D3 zoom, popup behavior, and expand/collapse graph reloads. New model graph pages should reuse this pattern contract for visual styling, report overlay placement, renderer math, and architecture schema ingestion.

layoutContract.notes: Graphviz source pages should add graph margin/pad and expanded-cluster margin before rendering so container title pills and left-side node priority tags have enough breathing room.

interactionContract.renderController: Call window.PtoModelGraphvizPattern.renderController(container, graph, options) when the product needs pan/zoom, focus, evidence hover, edge tags, or external phase/step linkage. Set interaction.wheelZoomWithoutModifier=true for graph pages that intentionally use direct wheel zoom; the default remains Command/Control-wheel so embedded previews can pass normal scrolling to their parent. The legacy render() API still returns an SVG and remains valid for static previews.
interactionContract.evidenceMap: Pass options.evidenceMap or graph.trainingEvidence keyed by node id. Evidence may include dimension, metric, what, description, evidence, action, sources, and relatedNodeIds.
interactionContract.edgeTags: Edges may define tag, edgeType, edgeTypeLabel, sourceAnchor, targetAnchor, curve, and tagPosition. The renderer places compact edge tags at a stable point along the rendered path.
interactionContract.hierarchyFocus: Selected nodes include explicit relatedNodeIds, evidence relatedNodeIds, ancestors, descendants for selected clusters, and directly connected edge neighbors. Focus changes must not refit or recenter the viewport.
interactionContract.selectableClusters: Set interaction.selectableClusters=false for pages where blank space inside expanded parent containers must pan only and must not open an inspector.

containerTitlePill: Use a filled pill for report-mapped container titles; do not add a second visible outline around the title.
Use the same fill as the priority tag: P0 red, P1 orange, P2 yellow.
Place the P0/P1/P2 tag inside the pill on the left of the title text.
Center the entire pill against the container bbox, clamp it within the container with an 8 graph-unit inset, and keep the pill above edge strokes in a root overlay layer.
Use SVG geometric text centering with dominant-baseline central for both tag and title text.
nodePriorityTag: When a normal node carries priority metadata, put the tag to the left of the node label and expand/reserve pill space when needed instead of drawing a separate external ring.
Change the original node pill stroke when emphasis is needed; do not add an extra visible border overlay.
reportOverlayContract.hitTarget: Expanded containers may add an invisible wide stroke hit target for clickability, but that hit target must not create a visible border.

variants.notes: Final source-checked DeepSeek V3.2 clean architecture schema: 61 main layers, dense 0-2, MoE 3-60, Module/Op/State node semantics, tensor facts on edges, explicit parameter nodes for embedding, attention projection, dense MLP, router, routed expert, shared expert, and LM head weights, no shapes or constraints inside node labels. Gallery tabs use deepseek_v32_gallery_modelviz.html so DeepSeek shares the same renderer, transparent clusters, neutral parameter nodes, light-mode typography, and readable fit rules as Qwen, openPangu, and Pangu MoE; the legacy deepseek_v32_modelviz.html remains available for full Graphviz inspection.

Infinite-canvas report overlay page using the source Graphviz iframe, transparent top header, right panel-shell inspector, Mapped Nodes graph focusing, red/orange/yellow priority fills, centered container title pills, and invisible-only enlarged border hit targets.
IDE-frame three-pane report overlay page using inline source graph, profiling JSON, operator source annotations, clearable selection state, and selected-edge dimming.

Clean Qwen-7B source/config architecture schema: 32 decoder layers, folded layer template, tensor facts on edges. Reference implementation of the current model-graph conventions: single top-to-bottom column, cluster boxes derived from visible-child bounding boxes that reflow/shrink on collapse, explicit left-side parameter nodes for embedding, attention, MLP, and LM head weights, right-side cache/state lane, top-right corner fold controls, --radius-xl cluster corners, transform-based cursor-anchored pan/zoom, single white selection stroke, and a top-left repeat-count badge.
Clean Gemma text-decoder source/config architecture schema: 30 decoder layers, folded attention/MLP/MoE structure, tensor facts on edges.

Source-checked openPangu-2.0-Flash full architecture schema: 46 main decoder layers, dense 0-1, MoE 2-45, DSA/SWA sparse MLA attention split, MoME local context paths, MTP branch with 3 next-token predictor layers, 60 nodes, 62 tensor/parameter edges, fused MoME causal-conv residual attributes on Q/KV/O local-context paths, and explicit parameter nodes for embedding, attention projection, dense MLP, router, routed expert bank, shared expert, LM head, and MTP head weights. The gallery adapter adds visual-only communication operators for the distributed execution path: attention/FFN AllGather and Reduce-Scatter plus MoE All-to-All Dispatch/Combine, all using sem:comm, and shows only the main decoder-block residual adds after attention and FFN. Its light-mode colormap is the shared model-architecture gallery profile: IO, state, output, parameter, and constant tensor nodes use raw neutral gray #D1D1D1, while semantic op/module colors use the Flash HSL profile. Parameter/weight tensors are left-side inputs; cache/state tensors are right-side auxiliaries.

Training-cross variant of the source-checked openPangu graph. The default L1 view keeps the Weight / Activation / Gradient / Residual / Optimizer cross visible with one folded Decoder Layer at the intersection. Its plus control expands that single representative Decoder Layer inline to L2 direct children only; Attention and FFN remain folded Modules rather than opening the complete model or deeper QKV/router/expert nodes. The main graph carries exactly 29 synthetic high-value telemetry values across token input, embedding, selected layer, final norm, loss, probe weight, gradient, sync, optimizer, and updated weight. Every telemetry record has step, microbatch, layer, owner rank, tensor scope, phase, and collection source; compact graph scope lines preserve those bindings while the right inspector expands Q/K/V, optimizer moments, quantiles, histogram, layer heatmap, and interpretation guardrails. This page opts into cursor-anchored direct wheel zoom while preserving native scrolling inside the inspector. Selecting a parameterized L1/L2 Module still binds Weight/Bias, Activation, Gradient, Residual, Optimizer, and parallel-mapping summaries without mutating canonical architecture facts.

Local Pangu MoE preview adapter for openPangu-R-72B and Pangu Pro MoE 72BA16B in the gallery. The 718B source schema remains bundled for direct inspection but is intentionally excluded from the tabbed preview because its expert-pool drill-down silhouette is not yet aligned with the other gallery models. The adapter reuses the shared model-graphviz renderer, preserves dashed parameter edges and tensor-style Parameter nodes on the left input lane, keeps the expanded routed-expert detail cluster sized through the terminal All-to-All Combine communication operator, and uses the shared model-architecture op-category color profile with communication operators in the blue-green lane.

useWhen: rendering model architecture graphs from Graphviz, TorchVista, or report-derived model nodes
showing collapsed module nodes with plus controls and expanded parent clusters without duplicate standalone parent nodes
keeping detail modules inside their semantic parent instead of drawing them as same-level peers
building graph previews where modules/ops use full-pill geometry and Input/Output/MTP tensor nodes use rounded rectangles
overlaying report diagnosis metadata on top of original Graphviz nodes without drawing separate visible rings
using an infinite-canvas graph with a transparent top header and right-side panel-shell inspector where mapped-node lists can focus the graph
allowedOverrides: graph data: nodes, edges, clusters, labels, type labels, and fixed positions
node dimensions when the Graphviz layout requires wider labels
glyph-only operation nodes via node.glyph and node.hideTypeLabel, for visual merge/add symbols that still retain canonical op semantics in the inspector
node semantic color keys, as long as colors are resolved through buildColorMap

Model architecture gallery previews should use window.PtoModelGraphvizPattern.modelArchitectureColormap(graph, options) and .pto-model-architecture-stage so DeepSeek, Qwen, openPangu, and Pangu MoE variants share the op-category semantic profile, communication operators use the blue-green lane, and all previews keep transparent parent containers, neutral selection stroke, hidden native fold toggles, consistent light-mode text/edge treatment, left-side Parameter/Weight inputs, and right-side State/Cache auxiliaries
IO colormap entries may use { raw: '#RRGGBB' } only when the page intentionally needs an exact fill such as a true neutral gray that must bypass HSL normalization

node fill colors only; light mode keeps semantic fills saturated enough for scanability and removes default op/module node borders; tensor, parameter, state, selected, and related strokes remain renderer-owned exceptions
stage width, height, and viewport padding
cluster boxes and local adapter layouts may add extra title-lane and external-edge clearance beyond child bounding boxes when expanded detail nodes sit near parent-flow operators

optional controller behavior through renderController: evidenceMap, edge tags with graph-node collision avoidance and perpendicular fallback offsets, external focus/phase linkage, hover rendering, selected/related classes, and pan/zoom fit options
collapseDepth URL overrides, while the default remains depth 2
report priority values P0, P1, or P2, using the documented red/orange/yellow fills
right inspector content and mapped-node list data, as long as graph focus behavior is preserved
forbiddenOverrides: rendering Input, Output, MTP, Constant, or Parameter tensor nodes as ellipses or full-pill op nodes
changing module/op rect node radius away from height / 2 full-pill geometry
placing expand buttons outside the right edge or over text
adding border strokes to clicked-operator preview cards
using light code/editor surfaces inside operator preview cards
using page-local hard-coded node colors instead of the shared semantic-first colormap and generated fallback palette
changing the shared colormap saturation/lightness or semantic default map without updating this pattern first
using pure red or pure green hue ranges for generated colormap entries
initializing a graph page with any selected node or selected operator state before user action
adding drop-shadow/filter shadows to graph nodes, clusters, fold controls, or graph panels
using colored or translucent expanded-cluster backgrounds instead of transparent parent containers, including repeated layer templates
using a parent cluster corner radius other than --radius-xl (16px)
placing expanded-cluster fold controls away from the top-right corner anchor
hard-coding non-node UI color, font, spacing, radius, border, or code-surface styles outside design-system tokens
dropping explicit op semantic colors; parent color is a fallback for nodes without a colorKey, not a replacement for sem:* node colors
rendering multiple parallel representative edges between the same folded source and target modules
recentering or refitting the whole graph after expand/collapse instead of preserving the clicked module's screen anchor
using dark edge strokes on the dark graph stage
reintroducing duplicate standalone parent nodes beside expanded clusters
placing report container title pills against the original label's left edge instead of the container bbox center
using blue for P2 report priority
using border-only color to distinguish report diagnosis cards or priority tags
drawing report priority as a separate visible outline around nodes or containers
letting a centered report title pill sit below edge strokes where the graph line crosses through the label
adding visible border styles to the panel-shell report inspector beyond the shared panel-shell token contract
agentReuseRule: Read this JSON first. For any model graph parity preview, use pattern.html because it iframes the real V3.2 Graphviz page. For model architecture gallery tabs, use the shared-renderer asset pages, including deepseek_v32_gallery_modelviz.html rather than the legacy DeepSeek Graphviz page. For new lightweight rendered examples, load pattern.css and pattern.js, then call window.PtoModelGraphvizPattern.render or renderController with data. Treat this as a rendered SVG pattern: node geometry, depth-2 default folding, top-right corner fold placement, right-inset expand placement, --radius-xl (16px) parent cluster corners, vertically centered node label+type stacks, explicit sem:* op colors before parent fallback, transparent expanded parent containers, stronger light-mode node-fill opacity without light-only font-size jumps or default op/module node borders, left-side Parameter/Weight input placement, right-side State/Cache auxiliary placement, Graphviz margin/pad, cluster margin, no-shadow graph rendering, edge tags that avoid graph node boxes with perpendicular fallback offsets, centered report-priority container title pills, no-extra-border priority tags, invisible-only click hit targets, no initial selected node/operator state, and semantic-first colormap generation with red/green forbidden hue ranges stay in the shared renderer rather than page-local CSS. New pages additionally derive cluster boxes from child bounding boxes with title-lane and external-edge clearance, use a single top-to-bottom decoder column, transform-based cursor-anchored pan/zoom, a single white selection stroke after user selection (no focus ring), and a top-left repeat-count badge.
